summary: Comprehensive UX audit across 375px / 768px / 1280px / 1920px viewports. Tested: homepage, /knowledge-center, /program, /compliance-library, article pages, /doctrine, /founder, /products/dq-file-builder. No horizontal overflow on any page. Content-page class (light background) confirmed on all article pages. Three known UX issues confirmed and measured precisely.

ui_bugs.issue: All nav text links (LaunchPath Standard, Compliance Library, Knowledge Center, Tools, About) have hit-area height of only 25px — well below 44px WCAG minimum. Links are rendered as <a> elements with fontSize=13px, paddingBottom=2px, no paddingTop. The navbar container is 56px tall but the clickable zone is ~25px. Only the Portal button meets 44px (45px). Fix: add paddingTop+paddingBottom to each link so the hit area is ≥44px (e.g., padding: 9.5px 0).

Hamburger menu is shown at 768px (breakpoint is 1100px). Industry standard is full horizontal nav at ≥768px. Confirmed known pending issue — desktop nav hidden at 768px, user gets hamburger drawer instead of inline nav. Fix: lower breakpoint from 1100px to 768px (or 800px) in Navbar.jsx CSS block.

ui_bugs.component: Knowledge Center — tab bar filters + Start Here strip
ui_bugs.issue: Tab bar category buttons ('All', 'New Entrant Program7' etc.) are 32–34px tall on mobile/tablet — below 44px. Start Here strip CTA link is 29–31px. Fix: increase min-height to 44px on tab buttons and the Start Here link.
ui_bugs.selector: KC tab buttons, Start Here link

ui_bugs.issue: Several inline anchor links on the homepage have very small hit areas: 'Review the full LaunchPath Standard' (16px h), 'Read the full story →' (20–22px h), 'See the AUTO breach map →' (25px h). These are plain text links with no padding. Fix: add display:inline-block with paddingTop/paddingBottom ≥10px, or wrap in a pill button.

integration_issues.issue: React hydration prop mismatch warning in CTAButton (SharedComponents.jsx:301). Server-rendered style differs from client-rendered style due to property ordering. Non-fatal (no visible crash), but causes hydration warning in console on /program and /knowledge-center.

issues: Body copy paragraphs on homepage are wider than 750px (measured 760–860px). The CSS rule '.prose, .article-body, .content-body { max-width: 68ch }' exists but most homepage section paragraphs are not wrapped in these classes. Need to apply max-width to body paragraphs in HomePage.jsx sections.

Article body paragraphs measure 780px wide — slightly above the ~750px guideline. The content-page .article-body p rule sets max-width: 68ch but the containing column appears to be slightly wider than 68ch at 21px base font. Consider reducing article container to max-width: 720px or ensuring the 68ch applies correctly.

KC index page shows 'Operational Library' heading with dark text on dark navy hero — the hero section does not have the .content-page override applied (hero shows as dark navy rgb(0,34,68)). The article pages themselves use content-page correctly. KC index hero may need the same data-testid='kc-hero' treatment.

confirmed_passing: No horizontal overflow on any page at 375px, 768px, 1280px, 1920px
Article pages (how-to-start, dq-file-requirements, corrective-action) all have content-page class → warm ivory background (#F6F3EE) with dark navy text — readability GOOD
Mobile hamburger menu displays correctly at ≤1100px; menu items have adequate touch targets (~44px row height in drawer)
Portal button in desktop nav meets 44px (45px measured)
Doctrine/Founder pages render dark navy background consistently — no overflow
action_items: HIGH: Fix desktop nav link tap targets — add paddingTop: ~9px to .desktop-nav a and .desktop-nav span so hit area reaches 44px (currently 25px)
MEDIUM: Lower Navbar.jsx hamburger breakpoint from 1100px to 768px so tablet gets full nav
MEDIUM: Add min-height: 44px to KC tab filter buttons and Start Here link
MEDIUM: Add paddingTop/paddingBottom to inline homepage text-links (Read the full story, Review LaunchPath Standard, See AUTO breach map) to reach 44px tap targets
MEDIUM: Wrap homepage section body paragraphs with max-width: 750px (or 68ch) container — currently 760–860px on wide screens
LOW: Fix React hydration warning in CTAButton (SharedComponents.jsx:301) — normalize style property ordering between server and client render
LOW: Check KC index hero section (currently dark navy) — ensure Start Here strip light-cream treatment is visible on mobile
critical_code_review_comments: Navbar.jsx line 98: navbar height is 56px but link padding is only paddingBottom:2px → actual clickable area = ~25px, not 56px. Links need explicit vertical padding to meet WCAG 2.5.5 (44px). Simple fix: add padding: '11px 0' to linkStyle() function.
Navbar.jsx line 323–329: breakpoint `max-width: 1100px` hides desktop nav — this is unusually high; standard tablet navigation at 768px+ should show full nav. Change to 767px.

index.css line 309: .content-page .article-body p { max-width: 68ch } — this only targets paragraphs inside .article-body. HomePage sections use custom inline styles. Need a global prose constraint for homepage body copy.
SharedComponents.jsx:301 CTAButton: inline style object property ordering differs between SSR/CSR — causes hydration mismatch. Move styles to a CSS class or use useMemo.
context_for_next_testing_agent: UX audit done for all major public pages at 375/768/1280/1920px. Main outstanding issues: (1) Nav links 25px tap height vs 44px required — fix in linkStyle() in Navbar.jsx. (2) Tablet hamburger shows at 768px due to 1100px breakpoint. (3) Homepage body copy >750px on 1920px. (4) KC tab bar + Start Here link < 44px. Article pages now correctly use light background (content-page class). No horizontal overflow anywhere. No critical crashes.
